title: AI copilots for sales teams
subtitle: Briefing to executive narrative
objective: Translate the sales copilot concept into an executive decision-ready deck.
context: Commercial teams want measurable productivity lift without adding more workflow complexity.
author: PPT Creator AI
key_messages: Sales teams lose time in repetitive drafting and follow-up work.
Managers need better visibility into execution quality.
A narrow workflow-first deployment reduces risk.
Measured adoption should come before broad expansion.

milestones.title: Diagnose workflow friction
milestones.detail: Choose the narrow workflow that already has strong executive pull.

milestones.title: Pilot with controls
milestones.detail: Test quality, adoption, and management visibility in a constrained rollout.

milestones.title: Scale with confidence
milestones.detail: Expand only after usage and outcome signals are strong enough.

options.title: Broad rollout immediately
options.body: This creates more noise and weakens the ability to measure real value.

options.title: Narrow rollout first
options.body: This keeps the workflow measurable and easier to govern.

faqs.question: Where should rollout begin?
faqs.answer: Start where workflow friction is already visible and where outcomes can be measured quickly.

faqs.question: How should success be measured?
faqs.answer: Use a mix of adoption, productivity lift, and execution quality improvements.

recommendations: Measure usage and outcomes before scaling
Focus leadership attention on execution quality rather than AI novelty
closing_quote: A strong briefing becomes more valuable when it is translated into a clear, decision-useful narrative.
